meta.marketNote: Markets open today — Thursday 26 June session
meta.trendLine: Sensex and Nifty trade in positive territory as markets recover from last week's IT-led selloff; gold eases slightly in Chennai; silver dips; Reliance Jio IPO process advances following DRHP filing; rupee strengthens as crude oil holds steady near $74.
meta.sodTitle: Markets Rebound — Sensex Near 77,100 as IT Stocks Recover; Jio IPO Process Advances Post-DRHP Filing
meta.sodBody: Indian benchmark indices traded positively on Thursday with Sensex near 77,100 and Nifty near 24,056, recovering from last week's Accenture-triggered IT selloff. Gold prices in Chennai eased to Rs 12,980/g (22K) from last week's Rs 13,600/g. Silver dipped to Rs 240/g. The Reliance Jio IPO process is advancing following the DRHP filing with SEBI on June 19, with analysts projecting India's largest-ever public offering timeline.

multibaggars.name: Ion Exchange (India) Ltd
multibaggars.thesis: Only listed pure-play water treatment company in India. PLI-era industrial demand + Jal Jeevan Mission municipal contracts = two independent growth engines.
multibaggars.risk: Order execution risk; lumpy quarterly results

multibaggars.name: Nesco Ltd
multibaggars.thesis: Owns Bombay Exhibition Centre (India's largest MICE venue) + IT park income. Massive land bank in Mumbai with near-zero debt.
multibaggars.risk: Event-driven revenue volatility; low trading liquidity

multibaggars.name: Clean Science & Technology Ltd
multibaggars.thesis: Global leader in MEHQ and BHA antioxidant chemicals with no domestic competitor. Export-driven with pricing power.
multibaggars.keyMetric: ROCE >30% | Net cash positive | 5Y PAT CAGR ~22%
multibaggars.risk: Single-product concentration; Chinese competition risk on margins

multibaggars.name: Hindustan Zinc Ltd
multibaggars.thesis: World's second-largest zinc-lead miner. EV battery demand and galvanising demand from infrastructure spending = structural demand upgrade.
multibaggars.keyMetric: Dividend yield ~6% | ROCE >35% | Net cash ₹14,000+ cr
multibaggars.risk: Commodity price cycles; government divestment overhang

multibaggars.name: Indraprastha Gas Ltd
multibaggars.thesis: Delhi NCR monopoly CNG distributor. EV risk overstated — CNG cars still the most practical affordable option for Delhi's 3M+ auto-rickshaw and taxi fleet.
multibaggars.risk: APM gas price policy risk; EV adoption acceleration

stories.title: Sensex recovers to 77,100, Nifty near 24,056 — IT stocks lead partial rebound after last week's Accenture shock
stories.why: Markets recovering their footing after a sharp selloff gives investors clearer signal on whether last week was a sector-specific event or a broader risk-off move.
stories.summary: The BSE Sensex traded near 77,100.47 (+0.39%) and the Nifty 50 near 24,056 (+0.18%) on Thursday, partially recovering from last Friday's 607-point Sensex crash triggered by Accenture's weak guidance. IT stocks — which bore the brunt of last week's losses — staged a partial recovery as investors assessed whether the Accenture warning reflected a sector-wide issue or a company-specific problem. Broader market breadth remained positive with small and midcaps continuing to outperform large-caps. USD/INR firmed to 94.71 as crude oil held near $74.10 per barrel following the US-Iran ceasefire holding.
stories.take: A recovery this orderly after a sharp single-day shock suggests last week's IT selloff was indeed a sector-specific imported shock rather than a broad risk-off event. Markets are telling you they believe Indian fundamentals are intact.

stories.title: Gold eases to Rs 12,980/g (22K) in Chennai — down from Rs 13,600/g last week as global demand cools slightly
stories.why: A Rs 620/g drop in 22K gold in Chennai in one week is significant for buyers planning jewellery or investment purchases — this week's rate is near June's lower range.
stories.summary: Gold rates in Chennai have eased this week with 22K gold at Rs 12,980 per gram and 24K at Rs 14,133 per gram, per Goodreturns data for June 26. This compares to Rs 13,600/g (22K) recorded last week, a meaningful drop of around Rs 620/g. Global gold prices have softened slightly as the US-Iran ceasefire reduces safe-haven demand and crude oil stabilises near $74/barrel. The rupee strengthening against the dollar (now 94.71) has also eased import cost pressure on gold prices in India.
stories.take: For Chennai households planning jewellery purchases ahead of the festive season or for investment, this week's rate near the lower end of June's range offers a better entry point than last week. The Rs 620/g drop is meaningful at scale.

path.title: Best time to buy gold — a practical guide

stories.title: Silver dips to Rs 240/g in Chennai — down from Rs 255/g as industrial demand softens
stories.why: Silver's dip this week diverges from gold's movement — the gap tells you this is driven by industrial demand factors, not pure safe-haven flows.
stories.summary: Silver in Chennai is priced at Rs 240 per gram today, down from Rs 255/g last week — a drop of Rs 15/g. Silver price in India is currently Rs 2,40,000 per kilogram, per Goodreturns. The softening reflects global industrial demand signals: silver's industrial uses (electronics, solar panels, EV components) are sensitive to global manufacturing activity, which has been mixed. India-specific jewellery and investment demand remains supportive, preventing a sharper drop.
stories.take: Silver at Rs 240/g is a more accessible entry point than last week for retail investors looking at precious metals. The divergence from gold's pattern signals this is a genuine industrial demand signal rather than sentiment — worth watching for a few more sessions before drawing conclusions.

stories.title: Reliance Jio IPO process advances post-DRHP — analysts model $60–70 billion listing valuation
stories.why: India's largest-ever IPO is on a defined timeline — and the valuation range being discussed would make it one of the top 10 largest IPOs in global history.
stories.summary: Following the DRHP filing with SEBI on June 19, the Reliance Jio IPO process is moving forward through the regulatory review phase. Analyst models are converging on a listing valuation of $60–70 billion for Jio Platforms, which would make it the largest IPO in Indian history by a significant margin and one of the top 10 globally. The IPO is expected to raise around $4 billion through a fresh issue. SEBI review typically takes 30–75 days, suggesting a potential listing window of August–October 2026 depending on regulatory pace.
stories.take: A $60–70 billion Jio listing would permanently reshape the Indian public market landscape — adding a company of this size to the exchange materially changes index composition, institutional allocation patterns, and retail investor exposure to the telecom sector.

stories.title: Crude oil holds near $74 as US-Iran ceasefire holds — rupee benefits; rate cut hopes rise
stories.why: Stable crude near $74 is a significant tailwind for India's current account deficit, inflation management, and RBI's ability to cut rates.
stories.summary: Brent crude oil traded near $74.10 per barrel on Thursday as the US-Iran interim ceasefire continues to hold. For India — which imports over 85% of its crude requirements — stable oil at $74 significantly eases the current account deficit pressure that had been building when oil spiked during the conflict. The stabilisation has also given the RBI more confidence on the inflation trajectory, with rate cut expectations for Q3 2026 firming up among economists. The USD/INR rate held steady near 94.71.
stories.take: Every dollar that crude oil stays below $80 is quietly improving India's macro fundamentals. If the ceasefire holds and oil remains in the $70–75 range through Q3, the RBI has a credible path to cut rates — which would be material for rate-sensitive sectors like real estate, banking, and auto.
